explanations.1: This declares that the XML file uses Microsoft Office's custom UI schema, which tells Office how to customize the ribbon (toolbar) in applications like Word or Excel.
explanations.4: The idMso attribute references a built-in Office tab by its internal name, and visible="false" hides that standard Home tab from the user.
explanations.5: insertAfterMso positions your custom tab right after another built-in tab (TabView) so users see it in a familiar location on the ribbon.
explanations.11: imageMso="AddFolderToFavorites" uses a pre-made icon from Office's icon library instead of you having to create or provide your own image file.
